Class Axis2JsonErrorResponse

java.lang.Object
org.apache.axis2.json.rpc.Axis2JsonErrorResponse
public class Axis2JsonErrorResponse extends Object
Structured JSON error response for Axis2 JSON-RPC services.

This is the canonical error envelope that every JSON-RPC error path serializes to the client. It replaces the previous pattern of returning HTTP 200 with {"status":"FAILED","error_message":"..."} inside domain-specific response objects (e.g. PortfolioVarianceResponse.failed()).

Error taxonomy

  • 400 BAD_REQUEST — malformed JSON or missing required envelope fields
  • 422 VALIDATION_ERROR — valid JSON, fails business-rule checks
  • 429 RATE_LIMITED — too many requests; retryAfter tells when to retry
  • 500 INTERNAL_ERROR — opaque; real details in server log keyed by errorRef
  • 503 SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE — downstream dependency or overload

The errorRef UUID is logged server-side for correlation while keeping client-facing messages safe from information disclosure (CWE-209). For 500/400, only the errorRef is exposed; for 422, the full validation message is safe to return since it describes the caller's own input.

Integration points

  • invalid reference 
    JsonRpcMessageReceiver
    — catches JsonRpcFaultException, sets this as RETURN_OBJECT + HTTP status on the outgoing MessageContext
  • invalid reference 
    JsonUtils#createSecureFault
    — builds this for parse/internal errors
  • OpenAPI spec — components/schemas/ErrorResponse mirrors this shape
  • MCP catalog — _meta.errorContract describes these fields

Wire format: 

{
  "error": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
  "message": "weights sum to 1.60000000, expected 1.0",
  "errorRef": "a1b2c3d4-...",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-04T12:00:00Z",
  "retryAfter": null
}
See Also:
  • JsonRpcFaultException
  • invalid reference 
    JsonRpcMessageReceiver#invokeService

    • Axis2JsonErrorResponse

      public Axis2JsonErrorResponse()

      public Axis2JsonErrorResponse(String error, String message, String errorRef, String timestamp, Integer retryAfter)

  • Method Details

    • validationError

      public static Axis2JsonErrorResponse validationError(String message)
      Build a validation error (HTTP 422).

    • rateLimited

      public static Axis2JsonErrorResponse rateLimited(String message, int retryAfterSeconds)
      Build a rate-limit error (HTTP 429).

    • serviceUnavailable

      public static Axis2JsonErrorResponse serviceUnavailable(String message, Integer retryAfterSeconds)
      Build a service-unavailable error (HTTP 503).

    • internalError

      public static Axis2JsonErrorResponse internalError(String errorRef)
      Build an internal error (HTTP 500). The message is kept opaque; full context is in server logs keyed by errorRef.

      Unlike validationError(), the errorRef is passed IN rather than generated here — the caller (JsonUtils.createSecureFault) generates it first so the same UUID appears in both the log line and the response.

    • badRequest

      public static Axis2JsonErrorResponse badRequest(String errorRef)
      Build a bad-request / parse error (HTTP 400).

    • getStatus

      public String getStatus()
      Always "FAILED" — read-only for backward compatibility.

    • getErrorMessage

      public String getErrorMessage()
      Legacy error message field. Same value as getMessage().

      public void setErrorMessage(String errorMessage)
